title: Confusion Matrix
description:
Build an NxN confusion matrix from true and predicted labels.

Entry `C[i][j]` = number of samples with true label `i` predicted as label `j`.

**Example:**
```
y_true = [0, 1, 2, 0, 1]
y_pred = [0, 2, 2, 0, 1]
C = [[2,0,0],[0,1,1],[0,0,1]]
```
constraints: Return numpy array of shape (n_classes, n_classes)
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def confusion_matrix(y_true: np.ndarray, y_pred: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    """Build NxN confusion matrix.

    Args:
        y_true: True labels (integers)
        y_pred: Predicted labels (integers)

    Returns:
        Confusion matrix of shape (n_classes, n_classes)
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def confusion_matrix(y_true: np.ndarray, y_pred: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true, dtype=int)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred, dtype=int)
    n_classes = max(y_true.max(), y_pred.max()) + 1
    C = np.zeros((n_classes, n_classes), dtype=int)
    for t, p in zip(y_true, y_pred):
        C[t, p] += 1
    return C
explanation: The confusion matrix reveals where a classifier confuses one class for another. Diagonal entries are correct predictions; off-diagonal entries are errors. It provides the full information from which precision, recall, and F1 can all be derived for any class.

test_cases.name: 2x2 binary
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
C = confusion_matrix(np.array([0,1,0,1]), np.array([0,1,1,0]))
assert C.shape == (2,2) and C[0,0]==1 and C[1,1]==1 and C[0,1]==1 and C[1,0]==1

test_cases.name: 3-class matrix
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
y_true = np.array([0,1,2,0,1])
y_pred = np.array([0,2,2,0,1])
C = confusion_matrix(y_true, y_pred)
assert C[0,0]==2 and C[1,2]==1 and C[2,2]==1

test_cases.name: Perfect predictions diagonal
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
y = np.array([0,1,2])
C = confusion_matrix(y, y)
assert np.all(C == np.eye(3, dtype=int))

test_cases.name: Shape is correct
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
C = confusion_matrix(np.array([0,1,2,2,1]), np.array([0,0,2,1,1]))
assert C.shape == (3,3)

hints: Increment C[true_label, pred_label] for each sample
